devotionals.title: A Brief History of Glory Presbyterian Church
devotionals.passageText: so that he might present the church to himself in splendor, without spot or wrinkle or any such thing, that she might be holy and without blemish.
devotionals.article: Glory Presbyterian Church was founded in 1881 and is recognised as the first Chinese Presbyterian church in Singapore. Here,

questions.text: Observation: Where do you see God's faithfulness in Glory's history?
Meaning: In 2026, our church announced that a church upgrading plan is in the pipeline. What is the significance of the two current buildings, built in 1887 and 1956, to Glory Church?
Application: How does remembering our church history deepen our thanksgiving to God?

devotionals.passageText:
1:1 Paul and Timothy, servants of Christ Jesus,

To all the saints in Christ Jesus who are at Philippi, with the overseers and deacons:

2 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

questions.text: Context // Philippi was a wealthy gold producing Roman colony in Macedonia. They worshipped the Roman Emperors as well as Egyptian and other gods. Paul first visited them around AD 49 (See Acts 16:1-40) and established there the 1st church in Europe. Paul wrote this letter while in prison in Rome about 13 years later in AD 62.

Observation // Who is writing to the Philippian Church? How are the authors described? (Is this surprising to you? Why?) Which 3 groups of people did they write to? What greeting does the author give to the church?

Meaning // The words saints in Christ means those who are holy and set apart by Jesus. Why do you think Paul calls the Philippian church by this title? How does this relate to his greeting of grace and peace after this? Where does grace and peace come from?

Application // From God and Jesus comes grace (undeserved favor) and the peace of God (4:7). As Paul reminds the Phlippian church of this, let us too be reminded and think about when in our lives have we experienced this grace and peace. Where will you need God's grace and peace this week? Take some time to praise God for His grace and peace.

devotionals.title: Purpose and Direction
devotionals.passageText: In their hearts humans plan their course, but the Lord establishes their steps.
devotionals.article:
We often spend considerable time planning our lives—mapping out careers, relationships, goals, and timelines. There's nothing inherently wrong with this; planning is wise and responsible. However, this proverb reminds us that ultimately, God's direction supersedes our plans.

This doesn't mean we should be passive or lazy. Rather, it invites us into a partnership with God where we plan intentionally but hold our plans loosely, remaining open to God's guidance and direction. Sometimes His path looks different from what we envisioned, but His way is always better than ours.

As you think about your life direction, ask yourself: Am I planning as if I'm ultimately in control? Or am I planning while staying open to how God might redirect my steps? Trust that even when your plans change, you're moving in the direction God intends.

questions.text: What plans are you holding onto most tightly right now? Why?
Can you think of a time when God's direction differed from your plans? What did you learn?
How can you better align your plans with God's will this week?
